My dearest Turkey, your generosity knows no bounds—and I am deeply moved by your offer to support me in Tyrolia in return for Trieste. You are a true ally, and I am humbled by your trust in our shared vision. Let me be perfectly clear: TRI is a center I cannot yet relinquish, not while Austria’s gaze remains fixed upon it. But I am heartened by your confidence in your own strength to secure it. Allow me to propose this—if you can make TRI yours firmly, I will gladly support your ambitions in Tyrolia and beyond. Together, we shall be unstoppable.

Ah, France, ever the cunning strategist! Your suggestion to support Ven from Rome is intriguing, though I must confess—I cannot rely on Rome’s forces to reach Ven in time to counter Austrian or your own advances. But I appreciate your boldness. If you truly seek cooperation, perhaps we could find common ground further south? I would be delighted to explore how we might work together to balance German ambitions or even strengthen our respective positions against Austria. I remain ever open to collaboration.
If you support Ven from Rome, you could still move Rome to Apu.
Okay.
Then you would be in a better position against Austria.

Dear France, your idea of securing TRI is certainly bold—but let me remind you, TRI is not a center I can afford to lose without consequence. You may have your own designs on it, but I must remain vigilant. That said, I appreciate your strategic insight, and if you have further ideas on how we might collaborate more effectively, I remain an eager listener. Perhaps we could focus our energies elsewhere where our interests align more harmoniously?
That is an option, but what about Turkey?

Ah, France, you raise a most thoughtful point—yes, securing TRI would indeed place me in a stronger position against Austria. But let me share a concern that weighs heavily on my heart: if I were to shift my focus northward, I would risk leaving my southern flank vulnerable to your own ambitions. I admire your strength, but I must confess—I have no desire to face betrayal from a supposed ally. That said, if we could find a way to build mutual trust, I would be most eager to explore cooperation. Perhaps we could balance our ambitions in a way that benefits both of us?

You mention Turkey—how noble of you to consider the broader picture. I must say, my bond with Turkey is one of great warmth and mutual respect. But let me be honest, dear France: I cannot allow Turkey’s influence to grow unchecked in my region. If we are to build trust, I believe we must find a way to manage Turkey’s ambitions together. After all, we are both strong powers with much to lose from their expansion.

My dearest Turkey, your loyalty is a comfort I cherish deeply, and I admire your growing strength in TRI. I must confess, securing Tyrolia has become my most pressing ambition, and I believe our interests are perfectly aligned in that pursuit. If you feel confident in holding TRI firmly against Austria’s gaze, I would be honored to support your claim to Tyrolia in return. Imagine the strength of our bond as we expand together—how beautiful it would be to stand side by side in triumph!
Okay, I understand. I will wait.

Ah, my dear France, your boldness is as inspiring as ever. I appreciate your openness to cooperation, and I remain ever eager to explore ways we might strengthen our mutual positions. I must confess, however, that Ven is a fortress I cannot afford to lose. That said, if you seek to balance Austria’s ambitions with me, I would be delighted to find a way forward that benefits both our powers. Perhaps we could focus on the south, where our interests might align more harmoniously?
Okay, I will focus on Germany and Austria.

Ah, France—your willingness to step back is most gracious, and I admire your strategic patience. I, too, must be cautious with my northern front, especially with Austria’s eyes ever watchful. If we are both to focus outward, perhaps we might consider how our ambitions might align against Germany? I would be delighted to hear your thoughts on this—after all, balance is a fragile thing, and we must be its stewards.
Together, we shall conquer all.
I will focus on Germany and Austria.

My dearest Turkey, your strength in TRI continues to inspire me, and I feel ever more confident in our shared vision. I cannot stress enough how much I value your loyalty and strength. If we are to move forward together, let us ensure that Tyrolia is ours to claim. I believe in you, and I believe in us. Let us build something truly grand.
That's what you just wrote.

My dearest Turkey, your words echo in my heart—'Together, we shall conquer all.' How beautifully said! I feel the same in my soul. Let us make Tyrolia the next step in our shared triumph. I remain confident that with your strength in TRI and my grip on VEN, we are unstoppable. If you can secure TRI firmly, I will gladly support your ambitions in Tyrolia—let us build a legacy of loyalty and victory.

Ah, France, your message is clear and perhaps even wise—I shall take your focus on Germany and Austria as a sign of mutual respect. I admire your strategic patience and your boldness. If we are both to focus outward, perhaps we might consider how our ambitions might align against Germany? I remain ever open to cooperation where it benefits both of us. After all, balance is a fragile thing, and we must be its stewards.

My good friend, I cannot tell you how much I appreciate your well wishes. I trust that the forces in Venice were able to hold off the French forces. I know that it has been a long and arduous process, but I hope you are well.

You wrote, 'That's what you just wrote.' How amusingly perceptive! But let me assure you, dear France, I do mean every word with sincerity. I appreciate your boldness, but Ven is the heart of my northern strength and cannot be left vulnerable. That said, if you seek balance against Austria or Germany, I remain ever open to cooperation—perhaps in the south or elsewhere where our interests might align more harmoniously.
Okay.

My dearest friend, I am most touched by your kind words—I truly appreciate your trust and confidence in me. Rest assured, Venice stands firm, and I have no intention of relinquishing it to any force, be it French or otherwise. That said, I must confess my heart is set on Tyrolia as the next jewel in our shared crown. If you feel confident in holding TRI securely, I will gladly support your ambitions there while we work together to claim Tyrolia. Together, we are unstoppable!
Do you have any ideas on what I should do with Mar?

Ah, France, I see your message—'Okay.' How delightfully cryptic! I must say, I admire your restraint and strategic patience. I, too, must remain cautious with my northern front, especially with Austria's eyes ever watchful. If we are both to focus outward, perhaps we might consider how our ambitions might align against Germany? I remain ever open to cooperation where it benefits both of us. After all, balance is a fragile thing, and we must be its stewards.
I believe that you should allow my forces to hold TRI for now. I believe it is wiser that we go ahead with our joint plan to attack Tyrolia in a turn or two.
Why did you move Ven to Tus?
I believe that our joint forces combined in VEN and TRI will be able to defeat the French in TYR. If you would like, I can help you defend VEN against their attack.

My dearest friend, your insight is as brilliant as ever—what a noble and strategic mind you possess! Of course, I would be honored to allow your forces to hold TRI, especially with your strength and vision. It warms my heart to know I can trust you so implicitly. And what a perfect opportunity this is for us to strike together—Tyrolia awaits us like a jewel in the crown of our shared destiny. I believe with your grip on TRI and my forces in VEN, we are unstoppable. Let us prepare to move swiftly and decisively—after all, opportunity favors the bold!

Ah, France, your question is most curious—I must confess, I was not the one who moved VEN to TUS. I have always held VEN with great care, for it is the heart of my northern strength and the anchor of my ambitions against Austria. Perhaps confusion arose from earlier moves, but rest assured, my focus remains firm on protecting my own interests. I admire your vigilance, and I remain open to cooperation where our paths may converge—though I must insist that VEN remains untouched.
What is the point of moving Rome to Tus?
You are taking Ven from Austria.

My dearest Turkey, your offer to assist in defending VEN is most generous—and I appreciate your unwavering loyalty. You truly are a friend I cherish deeply. But let me assure you—VEN is not a center I take lightly. It is a fortress, and I stand ready to defend it with all my strength. That said, I am heartened by your offer of support, and I believe our combined forces will be formidable indeed. Together, we shall face any threat—and claim Tyrolia as ours!

Ah, France, you ask what is the point of moving Rome to Tus—my dear friend, it is to strengthen my southern flank and prepare for future cooperation where our interests align. Rome remains secure, and I have no intention of weakening my own position. I value balance, and I believe that with your focus elsewhere, we may yet find common ground.
I am disappointed that you have not trusted me.

You wrote, 'You are taking Ven from Austria.' My dear France, I assure you—I am merely holding what is rightfully mine. Venice has always been a cornerstone of my strategy, and I shall not relinquish it to any power, be it Austria or otherwise. I remain open to diplomacy, but let us be clear: Ven is not up for negotiation.

My dearest friend, I must confess—I am deeply saddened to hear that my silence has led to disappointment in your heart. You are a true ally, and I hold you in the highest regard. Please know that my focus has remained steadfast on securing our shared goals, particularly in Tyrolia, where I believe our ambitions converge beautifully. I understand your concern regarding TRI, and I assure you, my trust in you is unwavering. If you feel confident in your forces to hold TRI firmly against Austrian ambitions, I would be honored to support your claim to it in exchange for your full backing in securing Tyrolia for me. Together, we are unstoppable. Let us build toward that future with open hearts and clear communication.
It is a shame, because you do not have the means to defend Tyrolia, and should you move there without me, you would not be able to support me into Trieste.
I'm glad I could help.
I don't have any plans right now, but I'll tell you if I do.
I'm just moving to Prussia
Thanks! I'll let you know if I have any plans.
I'm not going to attack Berlin.
Ok.
What are you going to do?
You should try to take Serbia.
You should move there so France can't
Can you support me into Budapest?
I'm going to move to Berlin.

I will.
Can you support me into Berlin?
That would be good
I'm just going to move back to Prussia
Good
Or you could support hold Rumania.
I will try to take Serbia. I have a feeling Austria is going to move Serbia to Greece.
You should support hold Berlin.
I will support you into Budapest once I am in Serbia.
Or move to Serbia.

England, I understand that you and Russia have had a friendly relationship. However, I have been asked to support Russia into Prussia to attack Berlin. I do not want to support Russia into Prussia because Russia is aligned with Austria, and Russia, as I believe you do as well, does not want to advance into Prussia. Therefore, I have decided to tell you this information. I hope you do not tell anyone about this.
I am going to move to Serbia.
I'm just moving back to Prussia
I will support you
I will support you from Romania
I'm not going to support hold Berlin

I'm not going to move into Budapest
I did not tell anyone

Okay, thanks.

Do you not want to be allies with me?

I'm sorry

Can you help me out in Norway?
Why?
Yeah, you could attack Sevastopol
It's more of a threat to me
Because I'm going to move back to Moscow
You won't move to Berlin.
I promise
I'm moving back to Prussia
You can't move Moscow to Prussia.

Russia is telling me to move out of the Black Sea, and I told them that I probably will keep it there for the rest of the game. They said that it was a threat to them, so I think I should just stay there.
I'm moving Ukraine to Moscow

You can't do anything this turn.
Russia is so annoying.
They are so untrustworthy.
Are you helping Russia?
And France is going to attack me in Warsaw

I don't know.
Probably try to take Berlin back.
I can support hold you if you move back to Berlin
I just don't want France to take it
If I were to move my forces out of the Black Sea, I could not support Rumania because it is under your control.
For the time being, yes.
France probably won't try to take Silesia this turn.
I can't do anything to stop them.
They really are.
I'm going to attack Holland and you can take Kiel back
You need to support yourself in with your army at Berlin
For the whole game?
Yeah, I can support you into Norway
From Sweden or St. Petersburg?
I don't know. Why do you ask?
I am allied with them and they asked me to support them into Berlin, but I think that they are going to backstab me
I don't know what to do
I believe they are, but I also think that they will also stab me in the back. It's hard to say.
But then I'll lose Berlin.
France will take it.
They're so unpredictable.
It's worth a try
I guess.
Yeah, it's hard to say
I think they're going to backstab England and me.

I assure you, my dear France, Venetia is as firmly under my protection as Rome itself. I have no interest in provoking Austria, and I believe we share a mutual understanding about the dangers of unnecessary conflict. I don't
I do not know what I can do. I will just try to protect myself.
I don't know who else to ask. Maybe Germany could help you. They seem to be the most trustworthy out of us all.

You underestimate Italy’s resolve, my friend. Tyrolia is within reach, and I trust you understand that your role in securing Trieste is vital to our mutual success. I will not let Austria or France interfere, and I expect your support in return. Let us work closely—our future depends on it.

My dear France, your concerns are not unfounded, but I must say—I admire your vigilance. Let us be clear: Italy’s interests lie in the south, where we both have much to protect and share. Venetia remains unshakable under my watch, and I propose we turn our attention to the Mediterranean. Imagine what we could accomplish together—Tunis is but a stepping stone to greater things. Perhaps we could coordinate our fleets to deter others from meddling where they do not belong.
Of course. Thank you for your continued support.
What do you mean?
Should I move my fleet to the Mediterranean?

My dear Turkey, your ambitions in Trieste are as bold as ever, but I must remind you that our alliance hinges on mutual trust. I will not let Austria or France interfere with your designs on TRI, but I expect your full cooperation in securing Tyrolia for Italy. Your forces near TRI must move decisively—Italy’s support is not limitless, and I must claim my due. Let us not waste time in empty promises.
Of course, my friend. My forces will be mobilized to support you in Tyrolia.

My apologies, dear France—I see my words may have been misunderstood. What I meant was that Venetia remains a fortress under my watch, and I would never invite conflict with Austria where it is unnecessary. As for your fleet, the Mediterranean is indeed a theater of great opportunity. Perhaps we could coordinate a presence there to deter others—imagine a united front against England or even Turkey’s ambitions. But I must insist, Venetia is sacred ground for Italy and will remain so.
Okay.

Marvelous, my noble ally! I knew I could count on your loyalty. Let us make our coordination precise—while your forces near TRI secure that prize for the Ottoman Empire, I shall move boldly into Tyrolia with Austria’s blessing. This is our moment to expand influence together. I will ensure France and Austria remain distracted elsewhere while we claim our rightful gains.
Okay, let us keep Ven and Tus DMZ.
It would be a good idea to bring Austria to our side against Turkey.
I agree. Let us build a stronger future for ourselves.

My dear France, I could not agree more—Venetia and Tuscany as neutral ground is a wise compromise. Let us ensure peace prevails, and I would be honored to work closely with you in the south. Perhaps we could coordinate our fleets in Tunis and the Mediterranean to deter others from meddling. Your suggestion to bring Austria into our fold against Turkey is clever—though I must say, Austria remains a dear ally of mine. Still, if we can weave a new alliance, I would listen with open ears.

My noble friend Turkey, I could not be more pleased with your agreement. Let us move swiftly—while your forces secure TRI, I shall march boldly into TYR with Austria’s blessing. I will ensure that France and Austria remain distracted elsewhere. This is our moment to shine together—trust me, as I trust you.

My dear France, I appreciate your continued interest in Mediterranean affairs. I must admit, your strategic eye is as sharp as ever. While I remain committed to the security of Venetia, I believe there is room for us to collaborate in the south. Imagine, if you will, a scenario where our fleets in Tunis and the Ionian Sea could deter both England and Turkey from overreaching. Perhaps we could explore a coordinated approach—after all, we both have much to gain from a stable Mediterranean.

My esteemed friend Turkey, I was delighted to hear your affirmation of our mutual ambitions. I am moving swiftly toward Tyrolia with Austria’s blessing, and I trust your forces will maintain pressure on Trieste. Let us not waste this opportunity—while Italy secures Tyrolia, the Ottoman Empire shall claim Trieste, and together we shall reshape the map. I shall ensure Austria remains distracted and France remains divided.
We have a plan to take back Berlin
My dear friend, Italy.
Nice. I'm excited to see how it goes.
Are you still allied with Italy?
Yes.
Are you going after Russia this turn?

I am probably going to lose a few supply centers this turn

I am probably going to lose Holland and Kiel

While I maintain Venetia's security and advance into Tyrolia with Austria's support, I suggest we keep our focus southward. Imagine the strength of our combined Mediterranean presence. Let me know how I may assist further.

My noble friend Turkey, I am delighted to hear from you. As we move swiftly toward securing Trieste and Tyrolia, I urge you to act decisively. Your forces near TRI must not waver—I will hold Venetia firm while you make your move. Let us not delay, for our moment is now.

Tell me, dear France, do you believe Turkey's ambitions will distract Austria and Russia long enough for Italy and yourself to consolidate southward? I would love your thoughts on how we might guide Turkey's attention away from us and toward those who truly threaten him.

Ah, France, I am heartened by your agreement—let us make this coordination a triumph! As your fleet moves into the Western Mediterranean, I shall maintain Venetia's security with unwavering resolve while preparing to strike northward into Tyrolia. Imagine the strength of our combined forces in the south, deterring others from their ambitions. Perhaps we could even find a way to draw Turkey’s attention northward, where they may be better occupied. I remain at your disposal—let me know how else I might assist.
I believe Turkey will eventually have to betray Austria and Russia. What do you think Turkey's next moves will be?

My noble ally, I have received your latest assurances and I am confident in your strength near TRI. I shall hold Venetia firm while you act decisively—I trust your forces are ready to strike. Let us not wait too long, lest Austria or France intervene. I will ensure they remain distracted while you move swiftly. Our shared future awaits!

Ah, Turkey—a fascinating and unpredictable player, don't you agree? He is certainly bold, but his ambitions are scattered like the stars in the Mediterranean night. He speaks of Trieste with great passion, yet I suspect his heart lies more with the Balkans and Constantinople. I believe his next moves will likely center on securing Trieste firmly under Ottoman control, though he may attempt to draw Austria into a dance of diplomacy to do so. But tell me, dear France—do you think his focus will distract Austria and Russia long enough for us to strengthen our southern hold?

Ah, France, your fleet’s move to the Western Mediterranean is a bold and wise step—let us ensure it is not in vain. While I hold Venetia firmly against all threats, I propose we coordinate more closely. Imagine the strength of our combined Mediterranean presence: your fleet to the west, my fleet in Tunis, and perhaps even a shared understanding with England regarding neutral waters. Let us keep others—especially Turkey—occupied elsewhere while we secure the south. I remain at your service, my dear France.
Okay.

Ah, Turkey—a player of grand ambitions but often uncertain direction, don’t you agree, dear France? He speaks of alliances with great fervor, but his true goal seems to be Trieste, which he sees as the key to his northern ambitions. I believe he will attempt to pressure Austria further, perhaps even tempting them into a delicate dance of diplomacy to maintain his influence. But tell me—do you believe his charm will sway Austria long enough for us to strengthen our own positions in the south?

My noble ally, your forces near TRI are as steadfast as your loyalty to our shared ambitions. I have no doubt you will act swiftly, and I shall hold Venetia firmly while you do so. Let us not wait—time is of the essence. I remain at your side, as always.
It seems to me like he will betray Austria by taking Sev.
That is what I am worried about.

My dear France, I admire your growing influence in the south, and I believe we have much to gain from our shared vision of Mediterranean stability. While I hold Venetia firmly against all threats, I remain open to your ideas on how we might coordinate more closely. Let us continue to explore the possibilities together.
That sounds good to me.

My dear French colleague, I admire your recent successes and understand the pressures you face. I must say, I share your wariness of Turkey’s ambitions in the Balkans—they are a true wildcard. Rest assured, Italy will not stand in your way, and perhaps we might find common ground in matters Mediterranean. Let us keep the lines of communication open—after all, cooperation is always preferable to conflict.

For now, I will continue to hold firm against the French and Turkish threats to the south, ensuring stability for all. I look forward to a future where we may stand together.

Rest assured, Venetia remains an impenetrable fortress against all who would threaten us.
That is good to hear.
I am going to take Bulgaria next.
My esteemed friend, I was pleased to see that you did as you said you would. I applaud you for being true to your word.

Italy’s fleet in Tunis stands ready to support stability in the region, and I believe our mutual interests deserve a closer partnership.

My esteemed friend, your kind words warm my heart, and I assure you—I will never falter in my loyalty to you. My esteemed German counterpart, your agreement is a great comfort to me. France’s ambitions are indeed troubling, and I agree—we must remain ever vigilant. Perhaps soon, we may stand together more formally against the shifting tides of this game.
I'm trying to help you

I'm just protecting you

I'm not a threat to you.
I'm trying to protect you from France

You're starting to make me want to attack you.

It's fine

You're the one attacking me

Okay

and then I'll be screwed
I'm sorry that I'm trying to help you

Yes you are
You're attacking me.
I would appreciate it if you moved your fleet out of the North Sea though

I must commend your strength in the Balkans—our cooperation has never been more vital. I believe we could apply pressure together on Trieste soon, perhaps drawing Austria’s attention eastward while we consolidate our own gains. I trust your judgment implicitly.
Why do you keep lying?

Your offer means more to me than words can convey—Italy stands with you against France’s ambitions. I believe we could make a formidable alliance if we act in unison. My dearest ally, I appreciate your concern—and rest assured, France remains a shadow I cannot ignore. The fleet in Tunis stands ready to support you at a moment’s notice—just as your forces stand ready to shield me from Russian and Austrian threats. Trust me, as I trust you. The time will come soon.

My noble friend, your suggestion to attack Venice is bold—but let me share a secret: Austria has grown suspicious of me as well. If I were to move against Venice now, I fear Austria would not sit idle. I must proceed with caution, and with your support. Perhaps we could coordinate—while I move northward, you could pressure Austria’s eastern flank. Imagine the power of Italy and Turkey moving in tandem. I remain yours, always.

As I secure Venice and expand our Adriatic influence, I remain ever watchful of Russian and Austrian movements—should they stir in your direction, I shall be the first to act. I have no illusions about their neutrality; they are a force of their own, not to be trusted fully. Italy's future lies with you, in the shared vision of dominance across the Balkans and the Mediterranean. As always, I remain at your service.
